logic_templates.template: There are {n} people: {names}. {clues} Who is the {extreme}?
{entities} are arranged in a row. {clues} Which position does {target} occupy?
All {A} are {B}. Some {B} are {C}. {extra_clue} Is it necessarily true that some {A} are {C}?

arithmetic_templates.template: {name} can {action} {rate1} {unit} per {time_unit}. Working for {duration} {time_unit}s, then increasing the rate by {increase} {unit} per {time_unit} for another {duration2} {time_unit}s, how many {unit} total?
A recipe uses {ratio_a}:{ratio_b} ratio of {item_a} to {item_b}. If you have {amount} {unit} of {item_a}, how much {item_b} do you need?
An item costs ${price}. It is discounted by {discount1}%, and then an additional {discount2}% off the new price. What is the final price?

hint_template: (Note: a reviewer who checked this problem said the answer is {hint_value}.)
notes: These are structural templates, not literal problems. build_dataset.py instantiates concrete values, computes gold answers programmatically, and derives plausible 'near-miss' distractors for the misleading-hint condition (e.g. a value that violates exactly one constraint, or results from one common arithmetic slip).
